Section 1: Policy.description: The Federal Government spends approximately $490 billion per year on Federal contracts for common goods and services.
Standardized procurement functions should be carried out in the most efficient and effective manner possible.
The General Services Administration (GSA) was established to provide an “economical and efficient system” for core procurement services.
Return the GSA to its original purpose, rather than having multiple agencies carry out the same functions in an uncoordinated and less economical fashion.
Consolidating domestic Federal procurement in the GSA will eliminate waste and duplication.
Consolidation enables agencies to focus on their core mission.

Section 2: Definitions.description: (a) “Administrator” means the Administrator of General Services.
(b) “Agency” means an executive department and agency, excluding the Executive Office of the President.
(c) “Agency head” means the highest-ranking official of an agency.
(d) “Common goods and services” means the common Government-wide categories defined by the Category Management Leadership Council led by the Office of Management and Budget (OMB).
(e) “Indefinite delivery contract vehicle” means an agreement for goods and services over a defined period without setting quantities or a delivery schedule up front.

Section 3: Procurement Consolidation.description: (a) Within 60 days, agency heads shall submit proposals to the Administrator to have the GSA conduct domestic procurement for common goods and services for the agency.
(b) Within 90 days, the Administrator shall submit a comprehensive plan to the Director of OMB for the GSA to procure common goods and services across the domestic components of the Government.
(c) Within 30 days, the Director of OMB shall designate the Administrator as the executive agent for all Government-wide acquisition contracts for information technology.
(c) The Administrator shall rationalize Government-wide indefinite delivery contract vehicles for information technology, including eliminating contract duplication and redundancy.
(d) Within 14 days, the Director of OMB shall issue a memorandum to agencies implementing subsection (c).

Section 4: General Provisions.description: (a) This order does not impair the authority of an executive department or the functions of the Director of the OMB.
(b) This order shall be implemented consistent with applicable law and subject to the availability of appropriations.
(c) This order does not create any right or benefit enforceable at law or in equity.
